title: herniaria glabra f. scabrescens
intro: Herniaria glabra f. scabrescens, also known as rough-leaved rasclet or rough-leaved rampion, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Herniaria glabra f. scabrescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
